---
title: "p-demotion-discourse"
author: "Cristian R. Juárez"
date: "2025-10-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)
library(viridisLite)
```


```{r}
mocCA190924 <- read.delim("/Users/cristian/Documents/postdocs/research/abst_pprs_docmnt/2025-pdemot-proposal/raw_data/mocCA190924_text.tsv", sep = "\t", header = T)
# glimpse(mocCA190924)
head(mocCA190924)
```

```{r}
mocCA190924 |> 
  filter(!is.na(val_type), val_type != "") |>
    count(val_type) |>
    mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) |>
  arrange(proportion) |>
ggplot(aes(x = reorder( val_type, proportion), y = proportion, fill = proportion)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
#  scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray90", high = "gray10") +
  labs(title = "Proportion of Each val_type",
       x = "val_type",
       y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))  # Tilt labels 45 degrees)

```

- Example to test correspondences between 

```{r}
# Filter out 'not_applicable' values
mocCA190924_filtered <- mocCA190924 %>%
  filter( P_arg != "not_applicable", P_arg != "",
         expression_type != "not_applicable", expression_type != "",
         !is.na(val_type), val_type != "",
        !is.na(P_arg),
         !is.na(expression_type))

view(mocCA190924_filtered)


# Create contingency table
contingency_p_expression <- mocCA190924_filtered %>%
  count(expression_type,val_type) |>
   arrange(desc(n))

contingency_p_expression

# I need to filter everything again before continuing
contingency_table_chi <- contingency_p_expression |>
count(expression_type, val_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = val_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames("expression_type")
contingency_table_chi

# Step 2: Run chi-squared test
chisq_result <- chisq.test(as.matrix(contingency_table_chi))

chisq_result


# Plot heatmap
ggplot(contingency_p_expression, aes(x = expression_type, y = P_arg, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  labs(title = "Interaction between P_arg and expression_type (excluding not_applicable)",
       x = "expression_type",
       y = "P_arg",
       fill = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()


# Table: verb_in_sentence vs val_type
contingency_verb_val  <- mocCA190924 %>%
  filter(!is.na(verb_in_sentence), verb_in_sentence != "",
         !is.na(val_type), val_type != "") %>%
  count(verb_in_sentence, val_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = val_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
 contingency_verb_val
```


```{r}
# Table: val_type vs P_arg
mocCA190924 %>%
  filter(!is.na(val_type), val_type != "",
         !is.na(P_arg), P_arg != "") %>%
  count(val_type, P_arg) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = P_arg, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
```


```{r}
mocCA190924 %>%
filter(!is.na(val_type), val_type != "",
         !is.na(P_arg), P_arg != "") %>%
  count(val_type, P_arg) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = P_arg, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(row_total = rowSums(across(where(is.numeric)))) %>%
  arrange(desc(row_total))

```


```{r}
# Load tidyverse
library(tidyverse)

# Assuming your filtered dataset is called `df_filtered`
# Step 1: Create contingency table
contingency_table <- mocCA190924 %>%
  filter(!is.na(expression_type), expression_type != "", expression_type != "not_applicable",
         !is.na(val_type), val_type != "") %>%
  count(expression_type, val_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = val_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames("expression_type")

as.matrix(contingency_table)
View(contingency_table)

# Step 2: Run chi-squared test
chisq_result <- chisq.test(as.matrix(contingency_table))

# Step 3: View results
chisq_result
```
